name: Content Card Grid
description: Responsive grid of registry content cards with clear type identity, concise trust signals, and action-forward metadata. Used for browsing patterns, themes, blueprints, and other registry items.

standard.description: Registry content cards with minimal badge usage, concise trust summaries, and a clean metadata footer. Grid: 1/2/3 cols at sm/md/lg.

slots.card-type-badge: Primary type badge with color-coded variant in CardHeader
slots.card-namespace: Namespace badge inline with the type, but visually quieter than the type
slots.card-title: Content name with _heading4 styling, clickable link to detail
slots.card-signal-row: Compact human-readable trust summary line for recommendation, showcase, or verification state
slots.card-meta: Version number, updated-at, and one optional action such as Open showcase

compact.description: Compact list-style content cards with minimal info. Grid: 1/2/4 cols. Useful for dense browsing views.

slots.card-icon: Content type icon on the left

editable.description: Content cards with edit/delete actions for the user dashboard. Includes status indicator and publish date.

slots.card-actions: Edit and delete Button group in CardFooter

slots.card-meta: Version, updated-at, and optional showcase action

visual_brief: Responsive grid of bordered cards that feel editorial and selective instead of overloaded. Each card leads with a type badge and a quieter namespace chip. The title and description are the main focus. Trust is conveyed through one compact signal row rather than a cloud of chips. The footer carries version, date, and a single useful action such as opening a showcase. The overall impression should be curated, calm, and high-signal: fewer pills, more hierarchy.

responsive.mobile: Single-column card stack. Cards take full width. Trust summary wraps to a second line if needed. Footer metadata folds naturally without horizontal overflow and action links stay readable.
responsive.tablet: Two-column grid. Standard card sizes with full descriptions visible and one compact trust summary row.
responsive.desktop: Three or four column grid depending on container width. Cards show title, description, trust summary, and footer metadata without chip overload.

layout_hints.card_treatment: Each card uses lum-card-outlined: transparent bg, colored border-left (3px) by content type — coral for patterns, amber for themes, cyan for blueprints, green for shells. Hover: border-color intensifies + translateY(-2px) + subtle shadow.
layout_hints.card_content: Keep the top badge row minimal — ideally type, namespace, and at most one additional high-signal badge. Move the rest of the trust story into a small summary line or footer metadata.
layout_hints.grid_layout: Responsive grid: 3 columns desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile. Gap: 1rem. Equal height cards per row. Avoid badge rows that force horizontal overflow.

composition.ContentCard: Card(d-surface, hoverable) > [CardHeader > [TypeBadge(d-annotation, color-coded) + NamespaceBadge(d-annotation)] + CardBody > [Title(heading4, clickable) + Description(text-muted, line-clamp-2) + SignalRow(text-muted)] + CardFooter > [Version(mono-data) + UpdatedAt(text-muted) + ShowcaseAction(optional)]]
